behavior_summary: Implements the 'U8P_MORDSTAT::look' (examine) event handler for a Mordea statue object. When an actor (typically the Avatar) looks at the statue, the script builds a short literal description string "statue of Mordea " and asks the item/process to 'bark' that string to the viewer.

Performs small setup control ops prior to producing the message (inferred 'set info' and 'process exclude' to adjust item state or visibility), allocates a temporary string, calls Item::bark with the constructed string and the item/process id, then frees the temporary string.
Captures the return value from Item::bark and returns it from the handler. A brief pop/suspend sequence appears before the final return (likely engine control/synchronization).
notable_control_flow: Pre-bark setup: set info and process-exclude style opcodes run (adjust item state/visibility) before string build.
Resource lifetime: allocate temporary string -> convert/obtain pointer -> push parameters -> call Item::bark -> free temporary string.
Result handling: return value from Item::bark is captured, pushed/used in an implies/pop pattern, then returned by the handler.
open_questions: Exact semantics of the 'set info' and 'process exclude' operations observed before the bark — what engine-visible state or display behavior do they change?
Precise meaning and expected values of the returned value from Item::bark and how the engine uses it after being returned by this handler.
The pop + suspend sequence's concrete runtime effect in the engine (timing, yielding, or cleanup) — inferred but not certain.

main_function.name: U8P_MORDSTAT::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to a 'U8P_MORDSTAT::look' (examine) event by presenting a short descriptive text for the Mordea statue to the viewer via the item's bark routine.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_MORDSTAT::look) — triggered when an actor inspects or looks at the statue/object

function_map.name: U8P_MORDSTAT::look
function_map.role: Event handler for the object's 'U8P_MORDSTAT::look' (examine) event; composes and dispatches a description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item method used to display or emit a short text string on behalf of an item/process to a viewer/process.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver/display the constructed description string ("statue of Mordea ") to the viewer/process; the item/process id (pid from [BP+06h]) is passed as the target 'this'/process for the call.

When an actor (typically the Avatar) looks at the statue, the script builds a short literal description string "statue of Mordea " and asks the item/process to 'bark' that string to the viewer.
